title: Power loss protection of data in memory devices
abstract: A system can include multiple memory devices and a processing device that is operatively coupled with the memory devices as well as with a controller device, and a sequencer device, where the controller device is configured to perform operations. The operations can include, in response to receiving a potential power loss indication signal, receiving a power fault interrupt detection signal, as well as synchronizing the power fault interrupt detection signal. They can also include sending one or more memory access commands to the sequencer device. The operations can also include executing the one or more memory access commands on a medium and stopping transmission of commands based on a power loss handling setting while executing the commands.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A system comprising: a plurality of memory devices; and a processing device, operatively coupled with the plurality of memory devices, a controller device, and a sequencer device, wherein the controller device is configured to perform operations comprising: responsive to a transmission of a potential power loss indication signal to the processing device, receiving a power fault interrupt detection; synchronizing the power fault interrupt detection signal; sending one or more memory access commands to the sequencer device; executing the one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device, on a medium of the memory device of the plurality of memory devices; and stopping transmission of commands based on a power loss handling setting while executing the one or more memory access commands. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein the processing device is further operatively coupled with a power loss detection module that is configured to: detect a voltage satisfying a threshold criterion; and transmit the potential power loss indication signal to the processing device in response to the voltage satisfying the threshold criterion. 3. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: sending, to the sequencer device, a concurrency table indicating a number of the one or more memory access commands to be concurrently executed on the medium of the memory device. 4. The system of claim 1, wherein the operations further comprise: monitoring the one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device. -27- 5. The system of claim 4, wherein the operations further comprise: responsive to the sequencer device completing the one or more memory access commands, sending a completion indication from the sequencer device to the controller device. 6. The system of claim 5, wherein the operations further comprise: responsive to receiving the completion indication, sending, by the controller device, a completion trigger to the processing device. 7. The system of claim 1, wherein stopping the transmission of commands based on the power loss handling setting comprises: stopping the transmission of commands between the processing device and the controller device or between the processing device and the controller device. 8. The system of claim 1, wherein stopping the transmission of commands based on the power loss handling setting comprises: stopping the transmission of commands between the processing device and the controller device and between the processing device and the controller device. 9. A method comprising: responsive to receiving, by a processing device, a potential power loss indication signal, transmitting a power fault interrupt detection signal to a controller device and to a sequencer device; synchronizing the power fault interrupt detection signal with the controller device; executing one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device on a medium of a memory device; and stopping transmission of commands based on a power loss handling setting while executing the one or more memory access commands. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: detecting, by a power loss detection module, a voltage satisfying a threshold criterion; and responsive to the voltage satisfying the threshold criterion, transmitting the potential power loss indication signal to the processing device. 11. The method of claim 9, further comprising: sending, to the sequencer device, a concurrency table indicating a number of memory access commands to be concurrently executed on the medium of the memory device. 12. The method of claim 9, further comprising: monitoring the one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising: responsive to the sequencer device completing the one or more memory access commands, sending a completion indication from the sequencer device to the controller device. 14. The method of claim 13, further comprising: responsive to receiving the completion indication, sending, by the controller device, a completion trigger to the processing device. 15. The method of claim 9, wherein stopping the transmission of commands based on the power loss handling setting comprises: stopping the transmission of commands between the processing device and the controller device, between the processing device and the controller device, or both between the processing device and the controller device and between the processing device and the controller device. 16. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium comprising instructions that, when executed by a processing device, cause the processing device to perform operations comprising: registering, by a processing device, a power loss handling instruction; sending one or more memory access commands, to a sequencer device; selecting a concurrency table indicating a number of memory access commands to be concurrently executed on a medium of a memory device based on a setting dependent on a status of a controller device; sending, to the sequencer device, the concurrency table; 

 executing the one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device according to the concurrency table, on a medium of the memory device; and stopping transmission of commands based on a power loss handling setting while executing the one or more memory access commands. 17. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 16, wherein the operations further comprise: monitoring the one or more memory access commands pending at the sequencer device; and responsive to the sequencer device completing the one or more memory access commands, sending a completion indication from the sequencer device to the controller device. 18. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 17, wherein the operations further comprise: responsive to receiving the completion indication, sending, by the controller device, a completion trigger to the processing device. 19. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 16, wherein stopping the transmission of commands based on the power loss handling setting comprises: stopping the transmission of commands between the processing device and the controller device or between the processing device and the controller device. 20. The non-transitory computer-readable storage medium of claim 16, wherein stopping the transmission of commands based on the power loss handling setting comprises: stopping the transmission of commands between the processing device and the controller device and between the processing device and the controller device.
